name: API Testing with Playwright
description: Use Playwright's built-in request context to test REST APIs directly, validate responses, and combine API calls with browser tests.

steps.title: Create an API test file
steps.description: Write tests using Playwright's APIRequestContext without a browser.

steps.title: Add authentication to API tests
steps.description: Set up a test fixture that includes an auth token for all requests.
steps.tip: Use custom fixtures to avoid repeating auth setup in every test file.

steps.title: Test API response schema
steps.description: Validate that API responses match the expected structure.

steps.title: Combine API and browser tests
steps.description: Use API calls to set up state before browser tests.

faq.question: How is Playwright API testing different from tools like Postman or Supertest?
faq.answer: Playwright's API testing integrates with the same test runner, fixtures, and reporting as your browser tests. You can mix API calls with browser interactions in a single test, share authentication state, and use the same assertions. No additional dependencies needed.

faq.question: Can I test GraphQL APIs with Playwright?
faq.answer: Yes. Use request.post with the GraphQL endpoint and send the query in the body: 'request.post('/graphql', { data: { query: '{ users { id name } }' } })'. Parse the response JSON and assert on the data field.

faq.question: How do I test file upload APIs?
faq.answer: Use request.post with multipart form data: 'request.post('/api/upload', { multipart: { file: { name: 'test.pdf', mimeType: 'application/pdf', buffer: Buffer.from('...') } } })'.
